author: jesswangpastry
body: Part 3: Perfectly Ice a Cake 🍰✨  Tools you’ll need (linked in my bio under the “shop” section): medium offset spatula, bench knife, and cake turntable   The frosting I’m using today is Swiss meringue buttercream which is silky smooth and perfect for piping intricate decorations! 🌸 KEY POINTS ✨ Make sure your icing doesn’t have large air bubbles (mix on low setting for at least 5 minutes before using) ✨ Start with a crumb coat to seal in crumbs (don’t add the extra crumby frosting back to the clean frosting!). ✨ Chill the cake for 15 mins between coats, no more because condensation will prevent the final coat from adhering properly. ✨ Always work from the top of the cake, to the sides, then clean the lip at the end                                                                                                                                                        ✨ Use a bench knife at a 90° angle to get that sleek, professional finish Do you want to learn different cake decorating techniques next? 🧁 If you want to elevate your baking skills from amateur to pro, then follow me for more real pastry education! 👩🏻‍🍳 #cakedecorating #cakeartist #homebaking #pastrychef #cakeart

author: jesswangpastry
body: Replying to @🫦  Viral Mango Cake Full Recipe!!! Mango Compote 100g mango puree 🥭 1/2 lemon juice 🍋 30g sugar 🍚 1 tsp pectin 1 vanilla bean or extract 🌿 1 small diced mango 🥭   Steps: 1️⃣ Mix sugar and pectin. 2️⃣ Boil mango puree and lemon juice. Add sugar-pectin mixture and cook until thick. 3️⃣ Let cool, then stir in vanilla and diced mango. 4️⃣ Pour into 1” dome molds and freeze. 5️⃣ Add more compote, stack frozen domes, and freeze again. Mango Mousse 150g mango puree 🥭 75g white chocolate 🍫 200g whipped heavy cream 🥛 6g gelatin sheets   Steps: 1️⃣ Bloom gelatin in water. 2️⃣ Melt half the mango with white chocolate, stir in gelatin. 3️⃣ Mix in remaining mango and fold in whipped cream. 4️⃣ Fill molds 2/3 with mousse, put in a mango sphere, freeze 1-2 hours. 5️⃣ Fill the remaining molds with mousse, put the frozen half on top, and freeze overnight. 6️⃣ Insert a skewer at the bottom. Chocolate Shell 100g white chocolate 🍫 100g cocoa butter 🧴   Steps: 1️⃣ Melt chocolate and cocoa butter, add yellow fat-based color. 2️⃣ Cool to 35-40°C (95-104°F). Dip frozen cakes quickly. 3️⃣ Wait for the shell to turn matte, then remove skewer. 4️⃣ Optional: add green/red for realism. 5️⃣ Chill for 30 minutes before serving.  Note: Feel free to swap the mango with other fruits and use matching molds to create different flavors!  If you want to elevate your baking skills, then follow me for more professional pastry education! #asmrdessert #asmrmukbang #fruitdessert #fruitcake

author: jesswangpastry
body: Baking Basics Ep. 13: Ganache 🍫   Ratios are by weight! For example, 1:1 would be 100g of chocolate and 100g of cream. Whipped Ganache 🖤 Dark: 1:2 (Chocolate : Cream) 🤎 Milk: 2:3 🤍 White: 1:1 ✨ Use: Light, airy filling or frosting — perfect for cakes & cupcakes!   Filling Ganache 🖤 Dark: 1:1 (Chocolate : Cream) 🤎 Milk: 1.5:1 🤍 White: 2:1 ✨ Use: Smooth, glossy layer for spreading or glazing!   Piping Ganache 🖤 Dark: 2:1 (Chocolate : Cream) 🤎 Milk: 2.5:1 🤍 White: 3:1 ✨ Use: Fudge, truffles, or bars — holds its shape beautifully!   Step-by-step to perfect ganache: 1️⃣ Heat the Cream: Bring your cream (or milk) to a simmer 🔥, then pour over chopped chocolate in a bowl. 2️⃣ Let it Sit: Wait 3-5 mins — this helps melt the chocolate gently 🍫 3️⃣ Stir Smooth: Gently stir until shiny and smooth ✨ 4️⃣ Cool & Use: For spreading or piping, let it cool at room temp. For whipped ganache, refrigerate until just firm, then whip until fluffy!  5️⃣ Decorate & Enjoy: Spread, pipe, or shape your masterpiece! 🍰 If you want to elevate your baking skills from amateur to professional, then follow me for more real pastry education! 🧑‍🍳

author: jesswangpastry
body: Baking Basics Ep. 12: Chocolate Tempering 🍫 Did you know? Tempered chocolate is set firm, shiny, and snaps when broken into. While untempered chocolate can look dull, streaky, and melt at the touch.  The easiest way to temper: The Seed Method 🌱 1️⃣ Gently melt most of your couverture chocolate over a double boiler, stirring until nearly smooth. 2️⃣ Remove from heat, then add your unmelted chocolate ‘seed’ and stir until cooled:    🤍 White chocolate: ~88–89°F (31–32°C)    🤎 Milk chocolate: ~86°F (30°C)    🖤 Dark chocolate: ~88–90°F (31–32°C) 3️⃣ Fish out unmelted chocolate pieces. 4️⃣ Test with a spatula—if it sets shiny and firm after a few minutes, it’s perfectly tempered! Professional Tips: 🥣 Always use a metal bowl and a double boiler for gentle, even heat. 💧 Keep all equipment dry water can cause the chocolate to seize. 🔥 Avoid reheating; if it thickens, just give it short, gentle bursts of heat. Overheating destroys the temper! 🍫 Use couverture chocolate not regular baking chocolate. ✨ Use polycarbonate molds for extra shiny chocolate work. If you want to elevate your baking skills from amateur to professional, then follow me for more real pastry education!

author: jesswangpastry
body: Chocolate DO’s & DONT’s from a Chef 🍫 ✅ Choose couverture chocolate for melting because it melts smoothly and gives a glossy finish ❌ Avoid chocolate chips or baking chocolate as they contain stabilizers that can make your chocolate grainy and uneven ✅ Use a metal bowl for melting since it conducts heat evenly and prevents overheating ❌ Avoid glass bowls, which trap heat unevenly and can cause seizing ✅ Melt your chocolate slowly using a double boiler as gentle heat helps keep it silky and prevents burning ❌ The microwave can heat unevenly and scorch your chocolate if you’re not careful ✅ Stir with a spatula for gentle folding that ensures a smooth, glossy texture ❌ Avoid using a whisk because it can incorporate too much air and disturb the perfect consistency If you want to level up your baking skills, then follow me for more professional pastry education! 🍰✨

author: jesswangpastry
body: Baking Basics Ep. 10: Chiffon Sponge Cake This is the perfect Asian bakery style sponge cake that’s light, airy, and not too sweet!   Ingredients: 🥚 4 large egg yolks 🍚 10g sugar for yolks + 70g for whites 🌿 4g vanilla extract 🥛 60g milk 🌻 40g vegetable oil 🍰 74g cake flour  🥚 4 large egg whites 🌿 4g cornstarch  Steps: 1️⃣ Whisk yolks with sugar, add milk, oil, and vanilla. 2️⃣ Sift in flour, combine gently. 3️⃣ Whip egg whites until soft peaks form with sugar and cornstarch (DON’T OVERWHIP!) 4️⃣ Carefully fold meringue into yolk mixture in thirds. 5️⃣ Pour into parchment lined oil sprayed pans immediately Bake at 325°F for 25 mins, then cool upside down.  Enjoy your homemade airy chiffon! 🌟  Tips & Tricks: 🔸 Recipe is perfect for two 6-inch cakes, a half-sheet roll, or double the amount for two 8-inch cakes.  🔸Customize flavor: Substitute some flour with cocoa powder or matcha. Swap vanilla for other extracts (ube, coffee, etc) 🔸 Don’t over mix or you’ll deflate your batter, once meringue is folded in work quickly to get the cake into the oven so the air you incorporated into the batter doesn’t escape. 🔸 Cool upside down to prevent sinking. And refrigerate overnight before layering for cakes!  If you want to elevate your baking skills, follow me for more professional pastry education!

author: jesswangpastry
body: Baking Basics Ep 8: Cooking Caramel 🧡 Dry vs. Wet method DRY METHOD Simply heat sugar directly in the pan until it melts evenly and reaches a beautiful amber hue. Great for: sauces, toppings, and quick caramelization. Tip: Stir gently if needed to prevent sticking, but avoid aggressive stirring to minimize crystallization. WET METHOD Dissolve sugar in water (and sometimes add corn syrup) then cook until caramelized. Great for: candies, delicate flavors, and when you need more control over caramel stages. Tip: Once boiling, do NOT stir. Instead, gently swirl the pan. To prevent crystallization, use a wet pastry brush to brush down any sugar crystals on the sides of the pan. General Tips for Perfect Caramel: ✨ Avoid stirring once the mixture boils, swirling distributes heat evenly. ✨ Use moderate heat, too high risks burning, too low prolongs the process. ✨ Patience is key, monitor the color carefully as caramel can turn quickly. ✨ If crystals start to form, brushing down the sides with a damp brush helps keep the caramel smooth. 🍬 Mastering caramel cooking is a basic technique that’s foundational for other recipes like toffee, caramel sauce, marshmallow, and more! If you want to go from an amateur baker to a professional chef, follow me for more baking education!

author: jesswangpastry
body: 3-2-1 Pie Dough Recipe 🥧  3 flour : 2 butter : 1 liquid Recipe for 2 (9”) crusts 🥣 12 oz (1.5 cups) AP flour 🧈 8 oz (1 cup) cold cubed butter 🧊 4 oz (1/2 cup) ice-cold water 🧂 pinch of salt How to Make Perfect Pie Crust ✨ 1️⃣ In a food processor, pulse the flour and butter until pea-sized. 2️⃣ With the processor running, stream in the cold water and add a pinch of salt. 3️⃣ When the dough starts to come together, stop and knead gently by hand. 4️⃣ Divide, wrap, and chill. Your homemade crust is ready to roll!  If you want to elevate your baking skills, then follow me for more professional pastry education 👩🏻‍🍳  #pastrychef #holidaybaking #pierecipe #homebaking #piecrust

author: jesswangpastry
body: Replying to @Devaᖭི༏ᖫྀ  Baking Basics Ep 1: My go-to cream for cake decorating 🎂 Reposting my Swiss Meringue Buttercream because we all need a refresh tutorial 🧈  Ingredients: Ratio: 1 part egg whites : 2 parts sugar : 3 parts butter (by weight) Note: 5 egg whites (150g) for a 6” cake, and 8 egg whites (240g) for an 8” cake 🥚 🔢 Steps: 1️⃣ Add egg whites and sugar to a mixing bowl. 2️⃣ Whisk together and heat over a double boiler until it reaches 140°F (60°C) or until the sugar is fully dissolved (rub between fingers to check). 3️⃣ Once heated, whip the egg whites until the bowl is cool to the touch. 4️⃣ Gradually add butter, 1 tablespoon at a time, whipping continuously. If it looks like it’s breaking, just keep going. Trust the process! 5️⃣ When smooth and fluffy, add a pinch of salt and a splash of vanilla for flavor!  💡 Storage Tip: Use it immediately or wrap it in plastic wrap and freeze it as buttercream pillows for up to 3 months. Just defrost and whip when you’re ready to decorate! wrap and freeze it as buttercream pillows for up to 3 months. Just defrost and whip when you’re ready to decorate!

author: jesswangpastry
body: Baking Basics Ep. 11: Pound Cake 🍰  Ingredients: 🧈 1 pound unsalted butter (softened) 🧂 1 pound granulated sugar 🥚 1 pound eggs (about 9 large, at room temp) 🌾 1 pound all-purpose flour (sifted)  Remember: it’s a classic 1:1:1:1 ratio — perfect for a traditional pound cake! Method: 1️⃣ Cream the butter and sugar together until light and fluffy—about 3-5 minutes. 2️⃣ Add eggs one at a time, beating well after each to keep the batter smooth. 3️⃣ Gently fold in the flour until just combined, don’t overmix! 4️⃣ Customize flavor at this point. 5️⃣ Pipe a line of butter down the middle of the batter to help it rise evenly. 6️⃣ Pour batter into a greased loaf pan and bake at 325°F for about 1 hour to 1 hour and 15 minutes, or until a toothpick comes out clean.  Tips: ✨ While still warm, soak the cake with simple syrup for extra moisture. ✨Finish with a classic glaze: liquid mixed with powdered sugar, for that perfect finish. ✨ Feel free to customize the flavor: add berries, cocoa powder, or spices to make it your own! Want to elevate your baking skills? Then follow me for more professional pastry education! 👩‍🍳

body: Having acne prone skin takes a lot of trial & error

Propaganda I fell for & regret
(acne edition): Using viral skincare (“viral” doesn’t mean good)
Not washing my face in the AM (pores became so clogged)
Going on accutane (my acne came back)
Pore clogging ingredients (everyone’s skin is different)
Using a niacinamide serum (SO irritating) 
Drinking energy drinks (the biotin caused cystic acne)
Drying out my skin (damaged my skin barrier)

author: ericanic0le
body: Just because some people have it worse doesn’t mean my medical struggles are less important and that my emotions aren’t valid. Lyme disease is a horrible condition, it's not easy to treat (especially when gone untreated for far too long) and most people live with debilitating symptoms for the rest of their life. Of course I am thankful I don’t have something like cancer, but again, that doesn’t mean someone’s diagnosis of chronic illness should be downplayed. And yes believe it or not, I’ve received a PLETHORA of comments like this 🤦🏻‍♀️ #lymedisease #lymediseaseawareness #chroniclymedisease #chronicpain #chronicillness #chronicillnessawareness
stickers: “Oh you think having Lyme Disease is bad? Some people are dying of cancer”

body: My debut single “One Thing He Wouldn’t Do” is officially YOURS FRIDAY, OCT 10!!!! Click the link in my bio to presave it NOW!  I’m so excited for the world to hear this song and message. I had the privilege of writing it with my friends @benjicowart and @thesethmosley, and I hope this song blesses you as much as it has my team and me over the last few months.  The little girl who used to write songs in her childhood bedroom and dream about the day her own music would be heard wouldn’t believe her life now. October 10th can’t come quick enough 🫶🏻 #artist #newmusic #newrelease #hollybrand #newartist

Said I was too far gone  He went over askin’ price
I was worth it in His eyes  He built a new foundation 
Became my cornerstone  His blueprint saved my life
His red blood washed me white  Yeah He stepped down from 
Heaven’s holy throne  To make this fixer upper His home.

stickers:
Here’s your reminder that 
Jesus is always holding on to
you, even if you aren’t holding

heart that’s torn in two Between the things 
I should and shouldn’t do Sittin’ on the fence

To do what’s right But I’ve been tempted 
Plenty times to give in Sometimes I did Through all the best
And worst of me Lookin’ back
It ain’t that hard to see What the truth is Even when I wasn’t
Holdin’ on to Jesus He was always
Holdin’ on to me

stickers:
✨midweek melodies✨ in a million ways I’m different
from who I used to be  then other days I still see
a lotta the same old me heard sermons ‘bout what’s right and wrong
and I try to stay on track  but when I take two steps ahead
I take another one back wish I always got it right
that’s the story of my life I know God is good and
one day He’ll make me whole but sometimes His word feels like
a little sandpaper on my soul I’m not who I wanna be
lotta miles on this road  but the love that left the 99
won’t let this 1 go  and He ain’t givin’ up 
on everyone of us  who’s still… a little rough around the edges

author: hollybrandmusic
body: “When the timing is right, I, the Lord, will make it happen.” Isaiah 60:22 I am SO EXCITED to officially announce I SIGNED WITH @integritymusic and @integritymusicpub!!!! Since moving to Nashville, I have prayed for the Lord to provide the right team at the right time, and I could not be more thrilled that He led me to Integrity. Words can’t express how thankful I am to be believed in and supported by this team, and I am honored to partner alongside them to facilitate the dream and vision for my music that God placed on my heart so many years ago. #myteambestteam 🥹🫶🏻 The work has only begun…we’re ready to bring y’all some MUSIC so soon!!! 🤠 Thank You, Jesus. Here we go!! 🎉 #artist #signed #integritymusic #signedartist #announcement #christianartist #countryartist #christianmusic #countrymusic #songwriter #singersongwriter

author: nobodywhoareu
body: What is it about the ocean that makes everything feel lighter? The moment I’m near it, something in me softens. The crashing waves, the salt in the air, the endless blue horizon — it all slows me down in the best way. My thoughts quiet, my shoulders relax, and I feel completely at ease, like I’m syncing with the rhythm of the tide. There’s no pressure, no noise, no expectations, just me, the sea, and a calm I can’t quite explain. Every time I leave the ocean, I feel like a different version of myself — more grounded, more present, and a little more in love with life. I truly believe I’m meant to live somewhere tropical, somewhere the air smells like coconuts and the only plans are watching the sunset or grabbing fruit from a roadside stand.

author: nobodywhoareu
body: What a year it has been! I’m so grateful for the community that supported me through my Accutane journey, where I could share and express my concerns. Looking back, I barely recognize that version of myself anymore. If you’re currently struggling with acne, I want you to know that one day you’ll reach your goal, and the struggle will feel like a distant memory. It won’t matter anymore. You are beautiful, and it’s not as big of a deal as it may seem right now. Love you!❤️ #accutane #clearskin #skinhealing #2024 #journey #fyp #foryou #motivation #confidence #healthy
stickers: 2024 - Skin healing journey in a nutshell ❤️‍🩹

author: ericanic0le
body: Will this be the era where we see the downfall of influencers??? 🤔🤔 I think if creators don’t begin to adjust the type of content they post, yes they will face the repercussions of that. There are creators who create content to actually connect with their audience and there are creators who create content with only one goal in mind — to get brand deals. As an influencer myself, I know that working with brands is how we make 98% of our income, but when every other video you post is an ad, people get turned off REAL quick. People also don’t want to constantly see you flaunt how much money you have when most people are struggling & working their butts off at their 9-5. Alix Earle & Emilie Kiser are the two influencers that come to mind who are extremely successful yet are very humble when it comes to their wealth. I think it’s awesome & inspiring to see successful creators on here, & seeing their videos on occasion of them treating themselves with a new designer bag or something is okay!!! Of course I continue to work with brands, this is my job, but I have become very picky because accepting every brand deal has already affected my growth and engagement in the past and I refuse to let that happen again. I also blame a lot of these brands for being so picky when it comes to the videos that influencers post. Every brand sends outs a brief and you have to follow the brief to a T! Very rarely will a brand allow a creator to have full control over the content which is sad. I think if more brands treated brand deals on TikTok & Instagram as they do on YouTube where the product is integrated into a video, the messaging would come off much more effectively & these brands would see larger conversion #influencers #influencermarketing #relatable #controversial #outoftouch
stickers:
WHY are influencers not
relatable anymore? 
(my pov as an influencer)

body: I wanted to share some of the diagnoses I’ve received over the last 2.5 while struggling with chronic illness! This is why it is so important to advocate for yourself & listen to your gut! #chronicillness #chronicpain #lymedisease #thoracicoutletsyndrome #dysautonomia

author: hollybrandmusic
body: Ever since I was a little girl, I dreamt of the day I would get to share my music with the world, and I can’t believe it’s finally here!! I often wondered what it would sound like, what I would want to say, and who it would be for. I truly feel that this song perfectly encapsulates exactly what the Lord has put on my heart to sing about. “One Thing He Wouldn’t Do” was written for the person who feels like they’re too far gone or has done too much for the Lord to love them. But here’s the truth: Jesus knew everything we would ever do and still chose to die on the cross. My prayer for my debut single is that every person who listens to this song is reminded that while Jesus would do a lot for them, one thing He would never do is give up on them. This release wouldn’t be possible without my precious husband, family, @integritymusic, @taylor_bradyy, @integritymusicpub, and @musiccitymanagement. I’m so thankful for each of you and feel immensely blessed to be surrounded by such incredible people.  Special thanks to my co writers, @thesethmosley (and OTHWD producer!) and @benjicowart, AND mixing genius @xoconnor for their collaboration on this song. Thank you for helping bring my vision to life!  GO STREAM ONE THING HE WOULDNT DO NOW!  #newartist #christianmusic #countrymusic #hollybrand #newrelease

author: hollybrandmusic
body: 2025 was one of my favorite years yet. The Lord was more than kind to us. I signed a record deal, wrote a ton of songs, sang all around the country, released my first single, celebrated two years of marriage, watched my husband take his football team all the way to the state championship in his first season as offensive coordinator, and so much more. Thanks 2025, and thank You Jesus! I have a feeling 2026 will be even better. 🫶🏻  #nye #the2025seasoncomestoanend  #2025 #newyear #highlightreel

body: Come hang out with us @bradleymountain in Columbia, TN THIS Saturday, December 6th, from 10am-12pm to celebrate our single release, “Tennessee Christmas”!! @Warren Peay and I will be sharing some of our favorite Christmas songs along with our single AND original music! author: hollybrandmusic
body: Mine and @warrenpeay’s cover of “Tennessee Christmas” is now available wherever you stream music!! 🤠 It’s such an honor to cover this classic originally recorded by the queen herself, @amygrantofficial, alongside my friend, @warrenpeay. Working on this project has been an absolute blast, and I’m so grateful for everyone who helped make this release possible!  I hope this song brings warmth, love, and coziness into your homes this holiday season. We’re sending you all the vibes of a tender Tennessee Christmas to wherever you’re listening from! Enjoy, Merry Christmas! 🫶🏻 #newmusicfriday #christmasmusic #hollybrand #christmascover #tennesseechristmas
